MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Amended)
Off Calendar May-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ATLANTIS TOWING INC., MOHAMMED ABUZAHRIYEH AND MARVIN LARIN'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion for summary judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

The record discloses that defendants inadvertently failed to attach several pieces of evidence cited in their separate statement of undisputed material facts. Defendants filed a notice of errata and an additional declaration to attempt to cure the defect. Plaintiff correctly notes that it is improper for defendants to introduce new reply evidence. (See CCP 437c(b)(4) ["The reply shall not include any new evidentiary matter, additional material facts, or separate statement submitted with the reply and not presented in the moving papers or opposing papers."].)

The preference is to hear motions for summary judgment on the merits where there exists a curable procedural default. (Walton v. Victor Valley Community College District (2026) 119 Cal.App.5th 1164.) Since the trial date in this case was recently continued to November 2, 2026, the court denies the motion without prejudice to allow defendants an opportunity to cure the technical defects in their motion.

Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
